parser_description: Crawl a domain locally (artifacts under output/), build a sitemap, infer likely API endpoints, and optionally ask OpenAI for additional URLs and analysis. By default this does not use any central coordinator — fleet mode uses coordinator.py separately.
url_help: Starting URL to crawl (http/https, or a bare hostname like example.com — https is assumed). Omit to run in batch mode using targets_file (see config) or --targets-file.
config_help: Path to JSON config file. Relative paths resolve under config/.
max_pages_help: Maximum number of pages to crawl before stopping.
model_help: OpenAI model name to use for AI-assisted stages.
openai_timeout_help: Timeout in seconds for each OpenAI request.
ai_probe_max_requests_help: Maximum number of AI-planned probe requests to execute.
ai_probe_per_host_max_help: Maximum number of AI-planned probe requests to execute per host.
ai_probe_delay_help: Delay in seconds between AI-planned probe requests.
sitemap_output_help: Path for the full sitemap JSON output. Relative paths resolve under output/.
condensed_sitemap_output_help: Path for the condensed sitemap JSON output. Relative paths resolve under output/.
url_inventory_output_help: Path for the URL inventory JSON output. Relative paths resolve under output/.
requests_output_help: Path for the requested-URLs JSON output. Relative paths resolve under output/.
evidence_dir_help: Directory where crawl and verification evidence files are stored. Relative paths resolve under output/.
crawl_wordlist_help: Path to a newline-delimited path wordlist used to seed spider requests (default: resources/wordlists/file_path_list.txt). Relative paths resolve from the repo root; bare filenames also search resources/wordlists/.
verify_timeout_help: Timeout in seconds for URL verification requests.
verify_delay_help: Delay in seconds between URL verification requests.
standalone_help: Local single-URL mode: fresh crawl (unless --resume), INFO logs on the console, all data under output/ only. Does not contact a central coordinator.
verify_urls_help: Verify discovered URLs with follow-up requests after the crawl completes.
crawl_delay_help: Base crawl delay in seconds for Scrapy requests.
max_delay_help: Maximum adaptive crawl delay in seconds.
backoff_factor_help: Delay multiplier applied after throttling responses such as 429/503.
recovery_factor_help: Delay recovery multiplier applied after successful responses.
verbose_help: Enable additional progress output during crawl, AI, and verification stages.
no_ai_help: Skip all OpenAI-assisted stages and generate only local artifacts.
session_state_output_help: Path for the crawl session-state JSON file. Relative paths resolve under output/.
resume_help: Resume from an existing crawl session-state file instead of starting fresh.
no_resume_help: Ignore saved session state and crawl from scratch (overrides config resume and --resume).
log_file_help: Path for the application log file. Relative paths resolve under output/.
log_level_help: Application log level for the log file (for example INFO or DEBUG).
console_log_level_help: Console log level for application logs.
scrapy_log_file_help: Path for the Scrapy log file. Relative paths resolve under output/.
scrapy_log_level_help: Scrapy log level (for example WARNING, INFO, DEBUG).
html_report_help: Generate an HTML report artifact for the crawl results.
html_report_output_help: Path for the HTML report output. Relative paths resolve under output/. Default: output/<root-domain>/report.html.
ai_data_output_help: Path for the AI endpoint-schema data file. Relative paths resolve under output/.
truncated_for_ai_help: Mark compact AI payloads as truncated when data is sampled down for model context.
no_truncated_for_ai_help: Disable the truncated_for_ai flag in compact AI payloads.
targets_file_help: Text file of target hosts/URLs (one per line, # comments allowed). Used when no url argument is given. Relative paths resolve against the repo root (nightmare.py directory).
batch_workers_help: Maximum concurrent domain crawls when running without a url (each crawl runs in its own subprocess). Overrides config batch_workers.
batch_state_dir_help: Directory for batch_run_state.json and per-domain lock files. Relative paths resolve under output/. Default: output/batch_state.
status_help: Print a quick aggregate status report from on-disk output artifacts and exit.
